name: Elementary Particle

phase_0.boundary_condition: The asymptotic boundary of the scattering experiment where incoming and outgoing states are defined and observed.

VINIT.justification: The uninitialized void state before any field excitation or particle creation occurs.

TANCH.justification: The closed boundary that contains the entire scattering experiment and defines the final observable states.

AFWD.justification: The directed transformation of a particle state evolving forward through spacetime.

AREV.justification: The reverse morphism representing an antiparticle propagating backward in time.

CLINK.justification: The sequential chaining of interaction vertices and propagators to form a complete scattering amplitude.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-referential interaction where a particle emits and reabsorbs its own field, recognizing its own identity.

FSPLIT.justification: The branching of a single high-energy photon into an electron and a positron.

FFUSE.justification: The recombination of an electron and a positron into a single high-energy photon.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative branch representing a physical particle satisfying the mass-shell condition.

EVALF.justification: The negative branch representing a virtual particle violating the mass-shell condition.

ENGAGR.justification: The paradice where multiple scattering paths and particle states are held simultaneously without resolution.

IFIX.justification: The permanent, append-only record of a definite macroscopic measurement outcome in the detector.

phase_3.void_description: The unexcited quantum vacuum state with zero particles and zero observable energy fluctuations.
phase_3.true_description: The on-shell physical particle state satisfying the relativistic energy-momentum dispersion relation.
phase_3.false_description: The off-shell virtual particle state existing only as an intermediate mathematical term in perturbation theory.
phase_3.both_description: The quantum superposition of multiple interaction paths and entangled states held simultaneously prior to measurement.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the quantum vacuum state as the source of the system.
Evolve the vacuum state forward in time to the interaction point.
Branch the initial high-energy photon into an electron on the T-arm and a positron on the F-arm.
Anchor the T-arm as the on-shell physical electron state.
Propagate the electron forward through spacetime.
Anchor the F-arm as the off-shell virtual positron state.
Propagate the positron backward in time as the CPT conjugate.
Recombine the electron and positron to recover the original high-energy photon.
Compose the sequential scattering amplitude from the vertices and propagators.
Apply the self-energy loop correction where the particle interacts with its own field.
Enter the quantum superposition of all possible Feynman diagram paths.
Record the final macroscopic detection event, collapsing the wavefunction.
Terminate the system at the asymptotic detector boundary.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Lagrangian density defining the field interactions and symmetries.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Gauge boson exchange mediating forces between fermion fields.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Hilbert space storing the complete state vector of the system.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Hamiltonian operator dictating the unitary time evolution of the state.
phase_5.alfs_store: Standard Model parameter tables and the Particle Data Group review.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Action quantized in units of the reduced Planck constant.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Coherent quantum superposition of all possible scattering amplitudes.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Definite macroscopic measurement outcome with localized thermodynamic entropy increase.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — Unitary evolution of the closed quantum system conserves von Neumann entropy during propagation.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → CLINK → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Elementary Particle
-- Fingerprint: sig=(7,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=13
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 7)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [6] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [7] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [8] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [9] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑳  | identity — self-imscription
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑠 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def elementary_particle_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the quantum vacuum state as the source of the system.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Evolve the vacuum state forward in time to the interaction point.)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch the initial high-energy photon into an electron on the T-arm and a positron on the F-arm.) / FFUSE [7] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Anchor the T-arm as the on-shell physical electron state.)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Propagate the electron forward through spacetime.)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Anchor the F-arm as the off-shell virtual positron state.)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Propagate the positron backward in time as the CPT conjugate.))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [7]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [7] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Recombine the electron and positron to recover the original high-energy photon.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [8] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the sequential scattering amplitude from the vertices and propagators.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑳)  -- [9] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Apply the self-energy loop correction where the particle interacts with its o...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Enter the quantum superposition of all possible Feynman diagram paths.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record the final macroscopic detection event, collapsing the wavefunction.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [12] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Terminate the system at the asymptotic detector boundary.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def elementary_particle_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (elementary_particle_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def elementary_particle_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (elementary_particle_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem elementary_particle_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
